_note: One object = one complete clinical session (e.g., one scan read by radiologist + AI).
_note_session_id: Required. Use your case ID, patient study ID, or any unique identifier. Do not use personal health information directly.
_note_baseline_s: Optional but strongly recommended. Average time a radiologist spends reading this scan type WITHOUT AI, in seconds. Used to compute Effort Loss (EL). Example: 15 minutes = 900.

decisions._note: AI event: the AI system analyses the scan and produces a diagnosis recommendation.
decisions._note_interaction_id: Required. Unique ID for this event.
decisions._note_timestamp: Required. ISO 8601 datetime. Used to compute interaction timing.
decisions._note_event_type: The AI action name — match what you defined in Pilot Setup.
decisions._note_latency_ms: How long the AI took to analyse the scan and return its recommendation (milliseconds).
decisions._note_correct: Was the AI recommendation correct? Set to true if the radiologist confirmed without modification; false if they overrode or corrected the AI.

decisions._note: Human event: radiologist reviews the AI recommendation.
decisions._note_event_type: Human action — radiologist opening and examining the AI output.
decisions._note_duration_s: How long the radiologist spent reviewing this case (seconds). Key field for HCL and D metrics.

decisions._note: Human event: radiologist confirms the AI diagnosis.
decisions._note_event_type: Confirmation = AI was correct. Override would indicate AI was wrong.

_note: Session where AI was wrong — radiologist overrides.

decisions._note_correct: AI was incorrect — radiologist overrode the recommendation below.

decisions._note_duration_s: Longer review time: radiologist spent more time scrutinising the incorrect AI output.

decisions._note_event_type: Override event — confirms the AI was wrong (correct: false on the AI event).
